response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with deep web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role requires SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear on the resume. No evidence of GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, deep-learning workload optimization, or silicon-level performance analysis. Educational background (BS CS) would require 8+ years of relevant technical experience, which is present in years but not in the relevant hardware/accelerator domain.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering experience with strong systems background
Some low-latency, high-throughput backend work (Sociomantic micro-services)
Exposure to embedded systems and C/C++ at National Instruments
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack, not hardware-software co-design

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/AI accelerator hardware experience
No deep-learning workload or GPU architecture expertise
Long tenure with some low-latency systems work
